definitions.text: An area; somewhere within an area. An open space, particularly a city square, market square, or courtyard. A street, sometimes but not always surrounding a public place, square, or plaza of the same name.
examples: He is going back to his native place on vacation.

definitions.text: An open space, particularly a city square, market square, or courtyard.
A street, sometimes but not always surrounding a public place, square, or plaza of the same name.
To put (someone or something) in a specific location.
examples: He placed the glass on the table.
to place someone on a pedestal

definitions.text: To earn a given spot in a competition; to rank at a certain position ().
examples: The Cowboys placed third in the league.
Run Ragged was placed fourth in the race.

definitions.text: To finish second, especially of horses or dogs.
examples: In the third race: Aces Up won, paying eight dollars; Blarney Stone placed, paying three dollars; and Cinnamon showed, paying five dollars.
